findings.evidence: Line 97 casts `P.characteristicLowerFaceExponent ... : ℕ` to `ℤ`; grep shows Mathlib names Nat-to-Int facts `Int.natCast_*` (e.g. `Int.natCast_nonneg`, `Int.natCast_inj`), while TauCeti `_eq_intCast` examples cast integer-valued terms such as `(G.maslovOℤ x : ℚ)`.
findings.fix: Rename this theorem to `characteristicLowerFaceExponent_natCast` or `natCast_characteristicLowerFaceExponent` and update its uses.
findings.issue: `characteristicLowerFaceExponent_intCast` names `(P.characteristicLowerFaceExponent ... : ℤ)` as an `intCast`, but the source term is a `ℕ`, so the API advertises the wrong cast direction.

findings.evidence: Line 126 casts `P.characteristicUpperFaceExponent ... : ℕ` to `ℤ`; the local use at line 142 depends on the misleading `_intCast` name.
findings.fix: Rename this theorem to `characteristicUpperFaceExponent_natCast` or `natCast_characteristicUpperFaceExponent` and update its uses.
findings.issue: `characteristicUpperFaceExponent_intCast` has the same nonstandard cast name: it casts a `ℕ` exponent to `ℤ`, not an integer to another target.

summary: Two cast theorem names use `intCast` for a natural-number exponent being coerced to `ℤ`; Mathlib/TauCeti convention reserves `intCast` for casts from `ℤ`, while Nat-to-Int casts are named `natCast`.
